Ceftriaxone 1g Vial.diluent: Water for Injection or 1% Lidocaine (IM only)
Ceftriaxone 1g Vial.rate_iv: Slow IV Push over 2-4 mins OR Infusion over 30 mins

Vancomycin 500mg Vial.rate_iv: Intermittent Infusion over at least 60 mins (Red Man Syndrome risk if faster)

Meropenem 1g Vial.rate_iv: IV Bolus over 3-5 mins OR Infusion over 15-30 mins
Meropenem 1g Vial.stability: Use within 2-3 hours at room temperature

Hydrocortisone 100mg Vial.rate_iv: Slow IV Push over 30 seconds OR IV Infusion
Hydrocortisone 100mg Vial.stability: Use within 3 days if protected from light

Pantoprazole 40mg Vial.rate_iv: IV Push over 2 mins OR Infusion over 15 mins

Piperacillin/Tazobactam 4.5g.rate_iv: Infusion over 30 mins (Extended infusion 3-4h preferred for severe infection)

Furosemide 20mg/2mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Slow IV Push over 1-2 mins (max rate 4mg/min to avoid ototoxicity)

Ondansetron 4mg/2mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Slow IV Push over 2-5 mins OR dilute in 50mL NS and infuse over 15 mins

Metoclopramide 10mg/2mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Slow IV Push over 3-5 mins (rapid injection causes anxiety/EPS)

Metronidazole 500mg/100mL Infusion.stability: Use within 24h at room temperature; protect from light

Dexamethasone 4mg/mL Ampoule.diluent: No reconstitution needed; dilute in NS/D5W for infusion
Dexamethasone 4mg/mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Slow IV Push over 2-3 mins OR dilute and infuse over 15-30 mins

Omeprazole 40mg Vial.diluent: 0.9% Sodium Chloride (reconstitute with 5mL, then dilute to 100mL)

Calcium Gluconate 10% (10mL Ampoule).rate_iv: Infusion over 10-20 mins (NEVER rapid IV — risk of cardiac arrest)

Potassium Chloride 1.5g in 10mL.diluent: MUST dilute before use — NEVER give undiluted IV
Potassium Chloride 1.5g in 10mL.rate_iv: Max 10 mEq/hour peripheral; max 20 mEq/hour via central line with monitoring
Potassium Chloride 1.5g in 10mL.stability: Use within 24h; do NOT add to hanging bags

Regular Insulin (IV Infusion).diluent: 0.9% Sodium Chloride — add 100 units to 100mL NS (1 unit/mL)
Regular Insulin (IV Infusion).rate_iv: Continuous infusion — titrate to blood glucose (typically 0.1-0.5 units/kg/hr)
Regular Insulin (IV Infusion).stability: Use within 24h; flush IV line before use (insulin adsorbs to plastic)

Heparin 5000 IU/mL.diluent: Add 25,000 units to 250mL NS (100 units/mL) for infusion
Heparin 5000 IU/mL.rate_iv: Continuous IV infusion — dose by weight-based protocol; monitor aPTT

Enoxaparin 60mg/0.6mL Syringe.diluent: Pre-filled syringe — SC injection only (NOT IV bolus)
Enoxaparin 60mg/0.6mL Syringe.rate_iv: Subcutaneous injection — abdomen preferred; rotate sites
Enoxaparin 60mg/0.6mL Syringe.stability: Store at room temperature; do NOT freeze

Norepinephrine 4mg/4mL Ampoule.diluent: Add 4mg to 46mL D5W or NS (concentration 80 mcg/mL)
Norepinephrine 4mg/4mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Continuous infusion via CENTRAL LINE only — titrate to MAP >65mmHg
Norepinephrine 4mg/4mL Ampoule.stability: Use within 24h; check colour — discard if brown

Dobutamine 250mg/20mL Vial.rate_iv: Continuous infusion — start 2.5 mcg/kg/min; titrate to response

Amiodarone 150mg/3mL Ampoule.diluent: Dilute in D5W ONLY (NOT NS — precipitates)
Amiodarone 150mg/3mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Loading: 150mg over 10 mins; then 1mg/min for 6h; then 0.5mg/min — use CENTRAL LINE for prolonged infusion
Amiodarone 150mg/3mL Ampoule.stability: Use within 24h; protect from light; avoid PVC containers

Hydralazine 20mg/mL Ampoule.diluent: Dilute with NS or Water for Injection
Hydralazine 20mg/mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Slow IV Push over 5 mins (Hypertensive emergency); monitor BP every 5 mins

Midazolam 5mg/mL Ampoule.diluent: Dilute to 0.5-1mg/mL with NS or D5W
Midazolam 5mg/mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Slow IV Push over 2-3 mins for sedation; continuous infusion for ICU

Atracurium 50mg/5mL Ampoule.diluent: Dilute in NS or D5W (0.5mg/mL for infusion)
Atracurium 50mg/5mL Ampoule.rate_iv: IV Bolus over 10-15 seconds for intubation; continuous infusion in ICU
Atracurium 50mg/5mL Ampoule.stability: Use within 24h if refrigerated; use within 8h at room temperature

Metoprolol 5mg/5mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Slow IV Push 5mg over 5 mins; max 3 doses at 5-min intervals

Human Albumin 20% (100mL Bottle).rate_iv: Infuse over 60-120 mins; rate adjusted based on clinical response

Magnesium Sulfate 50% (10mL Ampoule).diluent: MUST dilute — add to 250mL NS or D5W
Magnesium Sulfate 50% (10mL Ampoule).rate_iv: Loading: 4g over 20 mins (eclampsia); Maintenance: 1-2g/hour — monitor reflexes and respiratory rate

Labetalol 100mg/20mL Ampoule.rate_iv: Bolus: 20mg slow IV; or infusion 0.5-2mg/min for hypertensive crisis
